Scenario requires creating a disposable workspace copy of the full repository to test fresh install behavior. This is not feasible in automated sweep context due to: (1) repository size and copy time, (2) risk of destructive operations on live workspace, (3) hard guard prohibits improvising destructive test fixtures when create step fails. The /doctor memory fresh install bootstrap requires isolation that cannot be safely automated without risking production state.

Scenario requires a disposable workspace with an existing populated memory index, then editing three markdown files under .opencode/specs/, running /doctor memory in diagnostic mode, and reverting edits. Creating a disposable workspace with a populated index is not feasible in automated sweep context due to: (1) need to copy active resolved profile database, (2) risk of affecting live workspace if sandbox creation fails, (3) hard guard prohibits improvising destructive test fixtures when create step fails, (4) requirement to make real file edits that must be reverted safely.

Scenario requires a disposable workspace with a populated memory index and runs a full rebuild that takes 5-15 minutes per the ETA prompt. This is not feasible in automated sweep context due to: (1) need for disposable workspace with populated index (same issue as DOC-323/324), (2) explicit 5-15 minute runtime exceeds the 8-minute timeout guard in instructions, (3) hard guard prohibits improvising destructive test fixtures when create step fails. The long-pole rebuild is intentionally slow and requires interactive ETA prompt acceptance.

Scenario requires a disposable Docker-backed workspace with a populated memory index, recording pre-rebuild checksums, starting full rebuild, sending Ctrl-C after 30 seconds, waiting for ADR-001 settle, comparing restored DB checksums. Docker daemon is available (verified with docker info), but creating a disposable Docker-backed workspace with populated index is not feasible in automated sweep context due to: (1) complexity of setting up Docker container with full repository, (2) need to copy memory database and ensure workspace structure, (3) long-running rebuild operation with timed Ctrl-C, (4) hard guard prohibits improvising destructive test fixtures when create step fails.

Scenario requires creating a disposable low-free-space filesystem fixture (APFS image on macOS or loopback/container volume on Linux), copying workspace into fixture, running /doctor memory --incremental=true, verifying refusal before snapshot/mutation. Creating a low-free-space filesystem fixture is not feasible in automated sweep context due to: (1) complexity of creating and mounting APFS images with hdiutil on macOS, (2) risk of affecting host filesystem if fixture creation fails, (3) need to calculate exact disk space requirements (2x DB total), (4) hard guard prohibits improvising destructive test fixtures when create step fails. Pass/fail criteria explicitly allows SKIP when operator cannot create disposable low-free-space fixture on host.
